functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.get_platform_gpio
documentation:
Attempt to return a GPIO instance for the platform which the code is being
executed on.  Currently supports only the Raspberry Pi using the RPi.GPIO
library and Beaglebone Black using the Adafruit_BBIO library.  Will throw an
exception if a GPIO instance can't be created for the current platform.  The
returned GPIO object is an instance of BaseGPIO.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.setup
documentation:
Set the input or output mode for a specified pin.  Mode should be
either OUT or IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.output
documentation:
Set the specified pin the provided high/low value.  Value should be
either HIGH/LOW or a boolean (true = high).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.input
documentation:
Read the specified pin and return HIGH/true if the pin is pulled high,
or LOW/false if pulled low.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.set_high
documentation: Set the specified pin HIGH.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.set_low
documentation: Set the specified pin LOW.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.is_high
documentation: Return true if the specified pin is pulled high.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.is_low
documentation: Return true if the specified pin is pulled low.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.output_pins
documentation:
Set multiple pins high or low at once.  Pins should be a dict of pin
name to pin value (HIGH/True for 1, LOW/False for 0).  All provided pins
will be set to the given values.
Basic implementation of multiple pin methods just loops through pins and
processes each one individually. This is not optimal, but derived classes can
provide a more optimal implementation that deals with groups of pins
simultaneously.
See MCP230xx or PCF8574 classes for examples of optimized implementations.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.setup_pins
documentation:
Setup multiple pins as inputs or outputs at once.  Pins should be a
dict of pin name to pin type (IN or OUT).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.input_pins
documentation:
Read multiple pins specified in the given list and return list of pin values
GPIO.HIGH/True if the pin is pulled high, or GPIO.LOW/False if pulled low.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.add_event_detect
documentation:
Enable edge detection events for a particular GPIO channel.  Pin 
should be type IN.  Edge must be RISING, FALLING or BOTH.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.remove_event_detect
documentation:
Remove edge detection for a particular GPIO channel.  Pin should be
type IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.add_event_callback
documentation:
Add a callback for an event already defined using add_event_detect().
Pin should be type IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.event_detected
documentation:
Returns True if an edge has occured on a given GPIO.  You need to 
enable edge detection using add_event_detect() first.   Pin should be 
type IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.wait_for_edge
documentation:
Wait for an edge.   Pin should be type IN.  Edge must be RISING, 
FALLING or BOTH.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO.cleanup
documentation:
Clean up GPIO event detection for specific pin, or all pins if none 
is specified.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.BaseGPIO._validate_pin
documentation: helper functions useful to derived classes

Base class for implementing simple digital IO for a platform.
Implementors are expected to subclass from this and provide an implementation
of the setup, output, and input functions.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter.setup
documentation:
Set the input or output mode for a specified pin.  Mode should be
either OUTPUT or INPUT.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter.output
documentation:
Set the specified pin the provided high/low value.  Value should be
either HIGH/LOW or a boolean (true = high).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter.input
documentation:
Read the specified pin and return HIGH/true if the pin is pulled high,
or LOW/false if pulled low.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter.input_pins
documentation:
Read multiple pins specified in the given list and return list of pin values
GPIO.HIGH/True if the pin is pulled high, or GPIO.LOW/False if pulled low.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter.add_event_detect
documentation:
Enable edge detection events for a particular GPIO channel.  Pin 
should be type IN.  Edge must be RISING, FALLING or BOTH.  Callback is a
function for the event.  Bouncetime is switch bounce timeout in ms for
callback

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter.remove_event_detect
documentation:
Remove edge detection for a particular GPIO channel.  Pin should be
type IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter.add_event_callback
documentation:
Add a callback for an event already defined using add_event_detect().
Pin should be type IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter.event_detected
documentation:
Returns True if an edge has occured on a given GPIO.  You need to 
enable edge detection using add_event_detect() first.   Pin should be
type IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter.wait_for_edge
documentation:
Wait for an edge.   Pin should be type IN.  Edge must be RISING,
FALLING or BOTH.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.RPiGPIOAdapter.cleanup
documentation:
Clean up GPIO event detection for specific pin, or all pins if none 
is specified.
GPIO implementation for the Raspberry Pi using the RPi.GPIO library.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitBBIOAdapter.output

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitBBIOAdapter.input

Enable edge detection events for a particular GPIO channel.  Pin

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitBBIOAdapter.remove_event_detect
documentation:
Remove edge detection for a particular GPIO channel.  Pin should be

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitBBIOAdapter.add_event_callback
documentation:
Add a callback for an event already defined using add_event_detect().
Pin should be type IN.  Bouncetime is switch bounce timeout in ms for 
callback

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitBBIOAdapter.event_detected
documentation:
Returns True if an edge has occured on a given GPIO.  You need to 
enable edge detection using add_event_detect() first.   Pin should be 
type IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitBBIOAdapter.wait_for_edge
documentation:
Wait for an edge.   Pin should be type IN.  Edge must be RISING, 
FALLING or BOTH.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitBBIOAdapter.cleanup
documentation:
Clean up GPIO event detection for specific pin, or all pins if none 
is specified.

GPIO implementation for the Beaglebone Black using the Adafruit_BBIO
library.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitMinnowAdapter

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitMinnowAdapter.setup
documentation:
Set the input or output mode for a specified pin.  Mode should be
either DIR_IN or DIR_OUT.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitMinnowAdapter.output
documentation:
Set the specified pin the provided high/low value.  Value should be
either 1 (ON or HIGH), or 0 (OFF or LOW) or a boolean.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitMinnowAdapter.input
documentation:
Read the specified pin and return HIGH/true if the pin is pulled high,
or LOW/false if pulled low.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitMinnowAdapter.add_event_detect
documentation:
Enable edge detection events for a particular GPIO channel.  Pin 
should be type IN.  Edge must be RISING, FALLING or BOTH.  Callback is a
function for the event.  Bouncetime is switch bounce timeout in ms for 
callback

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitMinnowAdapter.remove_event_detect
documentation:
Remove edge detection for a particular GPIO channel.  Pin should be
type IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.GPIO.AdafruitMinnowAdapter.wait_for_edge
documentation:
Wait for an edge.   Pin should be type IN.  Edge must be RISING, 
FALLING or BOTH.
GPIO implementation for the Minnowboard + MAX using the mraa library

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.reverseByteOrder
documentation: Reverses the byte order of an int (16-bit) or long (32-bit) value.

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.get_default_bus
documentation:
Return the default bus number based on the device platform.  For a
Raspberry Pi either bus 0 or 1 (based on the Pi revision) will be returned.
For a Beaglebone Black the first user accessible bus, 1, will be returned.

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.get_i2c_device
documentation:
Return an I2C device for the specified address and on the specified bus.
If busnum isn't specified, the default I2C bus for the platform will attempt
to be detected.

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.require_repeated_start
documentation:
Enable repeated start conditions for I2C register reads.  This is the
normal behavior for I2C, however on some platforms like the Raspberry Pi
there are bugs which disable repeated starts unless explicitly enabled with
this function.  See this thread for more details:
  http://www.raspberrypi.org/forums/viewtopic.php?f=44&t=15840

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.__init__
documentation:
Create an instance of the I2C device at the specified address on the
specified I2C bus number.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.writeRaw8
documentation: Write an 8-bit value on the bus (without register).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.write8
documentation: Write an 8-bit value to the specified register.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.write16
documentation: Write a 16-bit value to the specified register.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.writeList
documentation: Write bytes to the specified register.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.readList
documentation:
Read a length number of bytes from the specified register.  Results
will be returned as a bytearray.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.readRaw8
documentation: Read an 8-bit value on the bus (without register).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.readU8
documentation: Read an unsigned byte from the specified register.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.readS8
documentation: Read a signed byte from the specified register.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.readU16
documentation:
Read an unsigned 16-bit value from the specified register, with the
specified endianness (default little endian, or least significant byte
first).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.readS16
documentation:
Read a signed 16-bit value from the specified register, with the
specified endianness (default little endian, or least significant byte
first).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.readU16LE
documentation:
Read an unsigned 16-bit value from the specified register, in little
endian byte order.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.readU16BE
documentation:
Read an unsigned 16-bit value from the specified register, in big
endian byte order.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.readS16LE
documentation:
Read a signed 16-bit value from the specified register, in little
endian byte order.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.I2C.Device.readS16BE
documentation:
Read a signed 16-bit value from the specified register, in big
endian byte order.

Class for communicating with an I2C device using the adafruit-pureio pure
python smbus library, or other smbus compatible I2C interface. Allows reading
and writing 8-bit, 16-bit, and byte array values to registers
on the device.

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.Platform.platform_detect
documentation:
Detect if running on the Raspberry Pi or Beaglebone Black and return the
platform type.  Will return RASPBERRY_PI, BEAGLEBONE_BLACK, or UNKNOWN.

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.Platform.pi_revision
documentation:
Detect the revision number of a Raspberry Pi, useful for changing
functionality like default I2C bus based on revision.

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.get_platform_pwm
documentation:
Attempt to return a PWM instance for the platform which the code is being
executed on.  Currently supports only the Raspberry Pi using the RPi.GPIO
library and Beaglebone Black using the Adafruit_BBIO library.  Will throw an
exception if a PWM instance can't be created for the current platform.  The
returned PWM object has the same interface as the RPi_PWM_Adapter and
BBIO_PWM_Adapter classes.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.RPi_PWM_Adapter

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.RPi_PWM_Adapter.start
documentation:
Enable PWM output on specified pin.  Set to intiial percent duty cycle
value (0.0 to 100.0) and frequency (in Hz).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.RPi_PWM_Adapter.set_duty_cycle
documentation:
Set percent duty cycle of PWM output on specified pin.  Duty cycle must
be a value 0.0 to 100.0 (inclusive).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.RPi_PWM_Adapter.set_frequency
documentation: Set frequency (in Hz) of PWM output on specified pin.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.RPi_PWM_Adapter.stop
documentation: Stop PWM output on specified pin.
PWM implementation for the Raspberry Pi using the RPi.GPIO PWM library.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.BBIO_PWM_Adapter

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.BBIO_PWM_Adapter.start
documentation:
Enable PWM output on specified pin.  Set to intiial percent duty cycle
value (0.0 to 100.0) and frequency (in Hz).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.BBIO_PWM_Adapter.set_duty_cycle
documentation:
Set percent duty cycle of PWM output on specified pin.  Duty cycle must
be a value 0.0 to 100.0 (inclusive).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.BBIO_PWM_Adapter.set_frequency
documentation: Set frequency (in Hz) of PWM output on specified pin.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PWM.BBIO_PWM_Adapter.stop
documentation: Stop PWM output on specified pin.

PWM implementation for the BeagleBone Black using the Adafruit_BBIO.PWM
library.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.PCF8574.PCF8574
documentation:
Class to represent a PCF8574 or PCF8574A GPIO extender. Compatible
with the Adafruit_GPIO BaseGPIO class so it can be used as a custom GPIO
class for interacting with device.

classes.name: MAX6675.MAX6675.MAX6675

methods.name: MAX6675.MAX6675.MAX6675.__init__
documentation:
Initialize MAX6675 device with software SPI on the specified CLK,
CS, and DO pins.  Alternatively can specify hardware SPI by sending an
Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDev device in the spi parameter.

methods.name: MAX6675.MAX6675.MAX6675.readTempC
documentation: Return the thermocouple temperature value in degrees celsius.
Class to represent a MAX6675 thermocouple temperature measurement board.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDev

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDev.__init__
documentation:
Initialize an SPI device using the SPIdev interface.  Port and device
identify the device, for example the device /dev/spidev1.0 would be port
1 and device 0.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDev.set_clock_hz
documentation:
Set the speed of the SPI clock in hertz.  Note that not all speeds
are supported and a lower speed might be chosen by the hardware.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDev.set_mode
documentation:
Set SPI mode which controls clock polarity and phase.  Should be a
numeric value 0, 1, 2, or 3.  See wikipedia page for details on meaning:
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serial_Peripheral_Interface_Bus

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDev.set_bit_order
documentation:
Set order of bits to be read/written over serial lines.  Should be
either MSBFIRST for most-significant first, or LSBFIRST for
least-signifcant first.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDev.close
documentation: Close communication with the SPI device.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDev.write
documentation:
Half-duplex SPI write.  The specified array of bytes will be clocked
out the MOSI line.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDev.read
documentation:
Half-duplex SPI read.  The specified length of bytes will be clocked
in the MISO line and returned as a bytearray object.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDev.transfer
documentation:
Full-duplex SPI read and write.  The specified array of bytes will be
clocked out the MOSI line, while simultaneously bytes will be read from
the MISO line.  Read bytes will be returned as a bytearray object.
Hardware-based SPI implementation using the spidev interface.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDevMraa

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDevMraa.set_clock_hz
documentation:
Set the speed of the SPI clock in hertz.  Note that not all speeds
are supported and a lower speed might be chosen by the hardware.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDevMraa.set_mode
documentation:
Set SPI mode which controls clock polarity and phase.  Should be a
numeric value 0, 1, 2, or 3.  See wikipedia page for details on meaning:
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serial_Peripheral_Interface_Bus

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDevMraa.set_mode
documentation:
Set SPI mode which controls clock polarity and phase.  Should be a
numeric value 0, 1, 2, or 3.  See wikipedia page for details on meaning:
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serial_Peripheral_Interface_Bus

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDevMraa.set_bit_order
documentation:
Set order of bits to be read/written over serial lines.  Should be
either MSBFIRST for most-significant first, or LSBFIRST for
least-signifcant first.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDevMraa.close
documentation: Close communication with the SPI device.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.SpiDevMraa.write
documentation:
Half-duplex SPI write.  The specified array of bytes will be clocked
out the MOSI line.
Hardware SPI implementation with the mraa library on Minnowboard

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.BitBang

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.BitBang.__init__
documentation:
Initialize bit bang (or software) based SPI.  Must provide a BaseGPIO
class, the SPI clock, and optionally MOSI, MISO, and SS (slave select)
pin numbers. If MOSI is set to None then writes will be disabled and fail
with an error, likewise for MISO reads will be disabled.  If SS is set to
None then SS will not be asserted high/low by the library when
transfering data.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.BitBang.set_clock_hz
documentation:
Set the speed of the SPI clock.  This is unsupported with the bit
bang SPI class and will be ignored.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.BitBang.set_mode
documentation:
Set SPI mode which controls clock polarity and phase.  Should be a
numeric value 0, 1, 2, or 3.  See wikipedia page for details on meaning:
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serial_Peripheral_Interface_Bus

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.BitBang.set_bit_order
documentation:
Set order of bits to be read/written over serial lines.  Should be
either MSBFIRST for most-significant first, or LSBFIRST for
least-signifcant first.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.BitBang.close
documentation: Close the SPI connection.  Unused in the bit bang implementation.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.BitBang.write
documentation:
Half-duplex SPI write.  If assert_ss is True, the SS line will be
asserted low, the specified bytes will be clocked out the MOSI line, and
if deassert_ss is True the SS line be put back high.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.BitBang.read
documentation:
Half-duplex SPI read.  If assert_ss is true, the SS line will be
asserted low, the specified length of bytes will be clocked in the MISO
line, and if deassert_ss is true the SS line will be put back high.
Bytes which are read will be returned as a bytearray object.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.SPI.BitBang.transfer
documentation:
Full-duplex SPI read and write.  If assert_ss is true, the SS line
will be asserted low, the specified bytes will be clocked out the MOSI
line while bytes will also be read from the MISO line, and if
deassert_ss is true the SS line will be put back high.  Bytes which are
read will be returned as a bytearray object.
Software-based implementation of the SPI protocol over GPIO pins.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase.__init__
documentation:
Initialize MCP230xx at specified I2C address and bus number.  If bus
is not specified it will default to the appropriate platform detected bus.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase.setup
documentation:
Set the input or output mode for a specified pin.  Mode should be
either GPIO.OUT or GPIO.IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase.output
documentation:
Set the specified pin the provided high/low value.  Value should be
either GPIO.HIGH/GPIO.LOW or a boolean (True = HIGH).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase.output_pins
documentation:
Set multiple pins high or low at once.  Pins should be a dict of pin
name to pin value (HIGH/True for 1, LOW/False for 0).  All provided pins
will be set to the given values.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase.input
documentation:
Read the specified pin and return GPIO.HIGH/True if the pin is pulled
high, or GPIO.LOW/False if pulled low.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase.input_pins
documentation:
Read multiple pins specified in the given list and return list of pin values
GPIO.HIGH/True if the pin is pulled high, or GPIO.LOW/False if pulled low.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase.pullup
documentation:
Turn on the pull-up resistor for the specified pin if enabled is True,
otherwise turn off the pull-up resistor.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase.write_gpio
documentation:
Write the specified byte value to the GPIO registor.  If no value
specified the current buffered value will be written.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase.write_iodir
documentation:
Write the specified byte value to the IODIR registor.  If no value
specified the current buffered value will be written.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP230xxBase.write_gppu
documentation:
Write the specified byte value to the GPPU registor.  If no value
specified the current buffered value will be written.

Base class to represent an MCP230xx series GPIO extender.  Is compatible
with the Adafruit_GPIO BaseGPIO class so it can be used as a custom GPIO
class for interacting with device.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP23017
documentation: MCP23017-based GPIO class with 16 GPIO pins.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.MCP230xx.MCP23008
documentation: MCP23008-based GPIO class with 8 GPIO pins.

modules.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H
documentation: Copyright (c) 2014 Adafruit Industries
Author: Tony DiCola

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.disable_FTDI_driver
documentation:
Disable the FTDI drivers for the current platform.  This is necessary
because they will conflict with libftdi and accessing the FT232H.  Note you
can enable the FTDI drivers again by calling enable_FTDI_driver.

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.enable_FTDI_driver
documentation: Re-enable the FTDI drivers for the current platform.

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.use_FT232H
documentation:
Disable any built in FTDI drivers which will conflict and cause problems
with libftdi (which is used to communicate with the FT232H).  Will register
an exit function so the drivers are re-enabled on program exit.

functions.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.enumerate_device_serials
documentation:
Return a list of all FT232H device serial numbers connected to the
machine.  You can use these serial numbers to open a specific FT232H device
by passing it to the FT232H initializer's serial parameter.

variables.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H_VID
documentation: Default FTDI FT232H vendor ID

variables.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H_PID
documentation: Default FTDI FT232H product ID

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.__init__
documentation:
Create a FT232H object.  Will search for the first available FT232H
device with the specified USB vendor ID and product ID (defaults to
FT232H default VID & PID).  Can also specify an optional serial number
string to open an explicit FT232H device given its serial number.  See
the FT232H.enumerate_device_serials() function to see how to list all
connected device serial numbers.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.close
documentation: Close the FTDI device.  Will be automatically called when the program ends.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H._write
documentation:
Helper function to call write_data on the provided FTDI device and
verify it succeeds.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H._check
documentation:
Helper function to call the provided command on the FTDI device and
verify the response matches the expected value.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H._poll_read
documentation:
Helper function to continuously poll reads on the FTDI device until an
expected number of bytes are returned.  Will throw a timeout error if no
data is received within the specified number of timeout seconds.  Returns
the read data as a string if successful, otherwise raises an execption.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H._mpsse_enable
documentation: Enable MPSSE mode on the FTDI device.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H._mpsse_sync
documentation:
Synchronize buffers with MPSSE by sending bad opcode and reading expected
error response.  Should be called once after enabling MPSSE.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.mpsse_set_clock
documentation:
Set the clock speed of the MPSSE engine.  Can be any value from 450hz
to 30mhz and will pick that speed or the closest speed below it.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.mpsse_read_gpio
documentation:
Read both GPIO bus states and return a 16 bit value with their state.
D0-D7 are the lower 8 bits and C0-C7 are the upper 8 bits.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.mpsse_gpio
documentation:
Return command to update the MPSSE GPIO state to the current direction
and level.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.mpsse_write_gpio
documentation: Write the current MPSSE GPIO state to the FT232H chip.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.get_i2c_device
documentation:
Return an I2CDevice instance using this FT232H object and the provided
I2C address.  Meant to be passed as the i2c_provider parameter to objects
which use the Adafruit_Python_GPIO library for I2C.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H._setup_pin
documentation: GPIO functions below:

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.setup
documentation:
Set the input or output mode for a specified pin.  Mode should be
either OUT or IN.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.setup_pins
documentation:
Setup multiple pins as inputs or outputs at once.  Pins should be a
dict of pin name to pin mode (IN or OUT).  Optional starting values of
pins can be provided in the values dict (with pin name to pin value).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.output
documentation:
Set the specified pin the provided high/low value.  Value should be
either HIGH/LOW or a boolean (true = high).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.output_pins
documentation:
Set multiple pins high or low at once.  Pins should be a dict of pin
name to pin value (HIGH/True for 1, LOW/False for 0).  All provided pins
will be set to the given values.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.input
documentation:
Read the specified pin and return HIGH/true if the pin is pulled high,
or LOW/false if pulled low.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.FT232H.input_pins
documentation:
Read multiple pins specified in the given list and return list of pin values
GPIO.HIGH/True if the pin is pulled high, or GPIO.LOW/False if pulled low.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.SPI.set_clock_hz
documentation:
Set the speed of the SPI clock in hertz.  Note that not all speeds
are supported and a lower speed might be chosen by the hardware.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.SPI.set_mode
documentation:
Set SPI mode which controls clock polarity and phase.  Should be a
numeric value 0, 1, 2, or 3.  See wikipedia page for details on meaning:
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serial_Peripheral_Interface_Bus

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.SPI.set_bit_order
documentation:
Set order of bits to be read/written over serial lines.  Should be
either MSBFIRST for most-significant first, or LSBFIRST for
least-signifcant first.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.SPI.write
documentation:
Half-duplex SPI write.  The specified array of bytes will be clocked
out the MOSI line.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.SPI.read
documentation:
Half-duplex SPI read.  The specified length of bytes will be clocked
in the MISO line and returned as a bytearray object.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.SPI.transfer
documentation:
Full-duplex SPI read and write.  The specified array of bytes will be
clocked out the MOSI line, while simultaneously bytes will be read from
the MISO line.  Read bytes will be returned as a bytearray object.

classes.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.__init__
documentation:
Create an instance of the I2C device at the specified address on the
specified I2C bus number.
Note that most of the functions in this code are adapted from this app note:
http://www.ftdichip.com/Support/Documents/AppNotes/AN_255_USB%20to%20I2C%20Example%20using%20the%20FT232H%20and%20FT201X%20devices.pdf

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice._idle
documentation: Put I2C lines into idle state.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice._transaction_start
documentation: Start I2C transaction.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice._transaction_end
documentation: End I2C transaction and get response bytes, including ACKs.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice._i2c_start
documentation: Send I2C start signal. Must be called within a transaction start/end.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice._i2c_idle
documentation:
Set I2C signals to idle state with SCL and SDA at a high value. Must
be called within a transaction start/end.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice._i2c_stop
documentation: Send I2C stop signal. Must be called within a transaction start/end.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice._i2c_read_bytes
documentation:
Read the specified number of bytes from the I2C bus.  Length is the
number of bytes to read (must be 1 or more).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice._i2c_write_bytes
documentation: Write the specified number of bytes to the chip.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice._address_byte
documentation:
Return the address byte with the specified R/W bit set.  If read is
True the R/W bit will be 1, otherwise the R/W bit will be 0.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice._verify_acks
documentation:
Check all the specified bytes have the ACK bit set.  Throws a
RuntimeError exception if not all the ACKs are set.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.ping
documentation:
Attempt to detect if a device at this address is present on the I2C
bus.  Will send out the device's address for writing and verify an ACK
is received.  Returns true if the ACK is received, and false if not.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.writeRaw8
documentation: Write an 8-bit value on the bus (without register).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.write8
documentation: Write an 8-bit value to the specified register.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.write16
documentation: Write a 16-bit value to the specified register.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.writeList
documentation: Write bytes to the specified register.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.readList
documentation:
Read a length number of bytes from the specified register.  Results
will be returned as a bytearray.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.readRaw8
documentation: Read an 8-bit value on the bus (without register).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.readU8
documentation: Read an unsigned byte from the specified register.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.readS8
documentation: Read a signed byte from the specified register.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.readU16
documentation:
Read an unsigned 16-bit value from the specified register, with the
specified endianness (default little endian, or least significant byte
first).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.readS16
documentation:
Read a signed 16-bit value from the specified register, with the
specified endianness (default little endian, or least significant byte
first).

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.readU16LE
documentation:
Read an unsigned 16-bit value from the specified register, in little
endian byte order.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.readU16BE
documentation:
Read an unsigned 16-bit value from the specified register, in big
endian byte order.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.readS16LE
documentation:
Read a signed 16-bit value from the specified register, in little
endian byte order.

methods.name: Adafruit_GPIO.FT232H.I2CDevice.readS16BE
documentation:
Read a signed 16-bit value from the specified register, in big
endian byte order.

Class for communicating with an I2C device using the smbus library.
Allows reading and writing 8-bit, 16-bit, and byte array values to registers
on the device.
